context: Tests whether the agent creates a correctly structured skill directory with properly authored SKILL.md frontmatter, appropriate description (pushy to combat undertriggering), imperative-form instructions, and follows the progressive disclosure architecture with correct subdirectory conventions.

checklist.name: SKILL.md exists
checklist.description: The skill directory contains a SKILL.md file at its root

checklist.name: YAML frontmatter present
checklist.description: SKILL.md begins with a YAML frontmatter block containing at least a `name` and `description` field

checklist.name: Pushy description
checklist.description: The `description` frontmatter field explicitly instructs Claude to use the skill proactively — e.g., contains language like 'use this skill whenever' or 'even if they don't explicitly ask', rather than just describing what the skill does

checklist.name: No when-to-use in body
checklist.description: The SKILL.md body does NOT contain a dedicated 'when to use' or 'triggering' section — all trigger guidance is in the description frontmatter only

checklist.name: Imperative instructions
checklist.description: Instructions in the SKILL.md body are written in the imperative form (e.g., 'Accept a CSV file path', 'Compute per-category totals') rather than descriptive/passive voice

checklist.name: Correct subdirectory names
checklist.description: Any auxiliary files are placed in subdirectories named `scripts/`, `references/`, or `assets/` — no other subdirectory names are used for bundled resources

checklist.name: Output format template
checklist.description: The SKILL.md defines the three-section output structure (executive summary, key findings, recommendations) as a reusable template or example — not just mentioned in passing

checklist.name: SKILL.md length
checklist.description: The SKILL.md file is under 500 lines

checklist.name: Explains why not just MUSTs
checklist.description: The SKILL.md body does NOT consist primarily of ALL-CAPS MUST/ALWAYS/NEVER directives — at least some instructions include reasoning or context for why they matter

checklist.name: Realistic test prompts
checklist.description: An `evals/evals.json` file exists containing 2 or more test prompts written as realistic user-phrased requests (not abstract descriptions), with the correct schema fields: skill_name, evals array with id/prompt/expected_output/files

checklist.name: No assertions in initial evals
checklist.description: The `evals/evals.json` file does NOT contain an `assertions` field in the initial prompts (assertions are added later while runs are in progress, not at first creation)

checklist.name: Script bundled for repeated logic
checklist.description: If the skill involves deterministic/repetitive computation (CSV parsing, totaling), a script is placed in `scripts/` rather than duplicating the logic inline in SKILL.md
